attacking_style: Pure sprinter who unleashes devastating acceleration in final 200m, thrives in chaotic finishes without organized lead-outs. Capable on uphill drags when following correct wheels.
team_tactics: Consistently performs without dedicated lead-out train or team support, relies on individual positioning and raw engine to navigate finale independently. Team provides minimal wind-splitting or organized sprint setup.
terrain_preference: Technical, chaotic finishes where organizational advantage is neutralized. Handles uphill sprint drags competently. Flat power suits wind-exposed classics (Brugge-De Panne final four).
vulnerability: Exposed when sprint organization collapses and requires improvisation (Pedersen incident). Tactical isolation becomes liability against coordinated lead-out trains in traditional bunch sprints.
key_patterns: Podiums repeatedly without team lead-out support across multiple stage races (4 observations)
Exceptional late-race positioning from compromised positions: 15th to 2nd acceleration documented
Pure engine overcomes tactical deficits in wind selections and chaotic finales
Mental resilience: won day after disappointment, handles high-pressure technical chaos effectively
Vulnerable when sprints require improvisation beyond raw speed (Pedersen case)
form_trajectory: positive: Multiple 2023 results show upward development at age 21, winning from chaos and making wind selections purely on power demonstrate maturing tactical awareness alongside raw speed
